(base64-encoded JSON)\n  ```\n- No dynamic code, user input processing, or network activity is present.\n\n---\n\n### Confirmations and Observations:\n\n1. **Presence of the code constructs:**\n   - All reports correctly identify the code as simple re-exports from local modules.\n   - The embedded source map comment is also consistently noted.\n\n2. **Malicious activity or malware:**\n   - All reports assign a malware score of 0, which aligns with the static nature of the code.\n   - No suspicious code, hardcoded secrets, or backdoors are detected.\n\n3. **Source map embedding:**\n   - The source map comment uses a data URI with base64-encoded JSON.\n   - This is common in build artifacts for debugging and not malicious.\n\n4. **Potential anomalies:**\n   - The only unusual aspect is the embedded source map, which is typical in minified or transpiled code.\n   - No report claims or implies any malicious intent based on this.\n\n---\n\n### Scoring and Logical Consistency:\n\n- All reports give a **malware score of 0**, which is appropriate.\n- The **security risk score** is 0 in all reports, consistent with the static, benign nature of the code.\n- The **obfuscated score** is 0, correctly indicating the code is not obfuscated or malicious.\n- The **confidence scores** are high (mostly 1), which is justified given the simplicity and clarity of the code.\n\n### Critical Evaluation:\n\n- **Are the scores reasonable?**  \n  Yes.
